findings: The hook frames quadratic equations as last module's polynomials with an equals zero on the right, giving students an immediate mental hook before any new method terminology shows up.
The overview slide names exactly three solving methods (factoring, completing the square, quadratic formula) plus the discriminant, in the order a real student would try them.
The concept slide pairs standard form with the zero-product property on a single frame, making explicit that the property requires zero on the right and showing the bridge from factoring to solving.
Method-specific slides each show a worked example next to the recipe so a student who freezes on a step has a concrete trace to compare against.
Both pause problems are solvable from material already shown, and each is followed by a visually distinct answer-reveal slide that shows every algebra step rather than only the final answer.

The rearrange-first trap slide directly targets the most common Algebra I quadratic error - applying the zero-product property to a product that equals a nonzero number - and the wrong / right columns highlight standard form as the gating step.
The discriminant slide gives students a way to predict the outcome before solving, which both saves time and gives them a self-check signal when an answer disagrees with the predicted count.

The closing path slide gives students concrete Learn Portal next actions (OpenStax reading, Khan Academy practice, dashboard assignment, advisor check-in) rather than generic encouragement, and previews Module 15 (Graphing Quadratic Functions) as the natural next step.
watch_points: Slide 6 (completing the square) packs six worked steps onto one frame; narration must move slowly and contact-sheet thumbnail should be re-checked at small size to confirm legibility.
Students who forget the +/- when square-rooting both sides will lose one solution silently. Flag for advisor follow-up before Module 15, where the two roots become the x-intercepts of the parabola.
